intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Echinalysium strictum." However, I found information on the plant Echinacea, which is commonly referred to as Echinacea stricta. 

Here is a paragraph based on the plant Echinacea:

Echinacea (Echinacea stricta) is a flowering plant of the daisy family, native to North America, commonly used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Echinacea include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea include alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Echinacea is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is commonly used in herbal medicine to prevent and treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
